register.note: Full-width (max-w-7xl) loss-surface portrait in the sanctioned panel with a mono figcaption — far more canvas-first than Ciechanowski's small floating watch under prose — but it loses a point because at thumbnail scale it collapses to an abstract red gradient with a faint line, so it is not yet poster-legible in isolation.

The bowl + zigzag trajectory is a real mechanism, but unlike Seeing-Theory's unambiguous residual squares the descent visibly converges into a dark lower-left (high-loss) corner rather than settling at the bright 'x' minimum, so 'the walk descends to the floor' is not legible from the pixels — held below floor.

Distill captions every regime inside the graphic (Ripples/Monotonic Decrease/Divergence + inline math); our hero is bare by design with start/end/minimum unlabeled and meaning living in adjacent prose, and even the working canvases carry less in-graphic signaling than Distill's labeled small-multiples.

The page is calm and Distill-soft, but the protagonist loss surface renders as coarse quantized bands upscaled with hard stair-stepped edges (canvas drawn at 1px/cell then CSS-scaled) — aliased and harsh next to Ciechanowski's buttery anti-aliased strokes — so the hero, the page's face, is the finish weak point.

Judged conservatively from statics: the hero performs exactly one explanatory ease-out path-draw (reduced-motion rests at the valley) and every training loop is learner-controlled with Play/Step/Step×10/scrub on a log-loss axis, so motion is transitional and steppable-where-complex, exceeding 3B1B's non-interactive frame on control.

The semantic grammar holds lab-wide and into AA-tinted prose (truth-gold data, prediction-blue line, param-purple eta/slider/path, error-red reserved for loss/'climb'/bullets, weight+underline so hue isn't alone); docked from 4 because the all-red hero band field uses error-red as ambient terrain, mildly diluting red's 'error' meaning.

hero.note: Full-width loss-surface specimen with a single ease-out load motion and a mono figcaption, but it carries no in-graphic labels (start/end/minimum unmarked), the path converges into a dark high-loss corner instead of the bright 'x', and it does not survive thumbnail scale.

assessment.note: The Explain act is a graded instrument with a live coordinated descent widget; transfer work is genuinely interactive (item 04 PREDICT & VERIFY sets eta=0.12 against the live walk, item 05 LAB TASK is hands-completed) — though the explicitly-labelled item 06 'TRANSFER' is itself a 3-option MCQ.

blocking: Re-render the loss surface as a smooth/anti-aliased (higher-resolution or true contour-line) field instead of 1px-per-cell hard-upscaled bands, to clear atmosphere-finish.
Make the hero descent visibly terminate at the bright minimum ('x') with the path moving from high to low loss, so mechanism-in-the-picture reads without prior knowledge.
Add minimal in-graphic annotation to the hero (start dot, 'minimum' at the x, a 'loss' legend) so meaning does not live only in adjacent prose.
verdict.summary: Strong canvas-first structure, disciplined colour, and explanatory motion, but the hero's harsh banded render and illegible descent endpoint hold mechanism, annotation, and atmosphere below floor 3 — fix the hero surface and it advances.
